market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+27.8% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+104.6% YTD) | 52W leader: information-technology (+46.8% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+25.5% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: communication-services, financials, healthcare | Régions en difficulté: germany, india, south-africa | 4 secteurs avec 6M négatif (momentum moyen-terme dégradé)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: information-technology(favored,b=1.57,d=0.68%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.22,d=0.44%), communication-services(neutral,b=1.21,d=-0.25%), financials(neutral,b=0.86,d=1.39%), real-estate(favored,b=0.37,d=0.78%), industrials(favored,b=0.97,d=0.62%), energy(favored,b=-0.17,d=0.92%), materials(favored,b=0.82,d=1.98%), healthcare(neutral,b=0.49,d=-0.18%), consumer-discretionary(neutral,b=1.18,d=-0.06%), utilities(favored,b=0.37,d=0.3%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Mixed signals: PCE at 3.77% runs hot but CPI at 2.4% and breakevens at 2.39% suggest inflation moderating; VIX 22 falling, positive yield curve (40bps), HY spreads tight at 278bps, and strong equity momentum (S&P +1.8% today) offset by Fed on hold and elevated real rates. No clear directional regime — leaning slightly risk-on but PCE divergence and gold at $4363 signal lingering uncertainty.

adjustments.action: Increase energy cap on strong momentum and elevated Brent
adjustments.rationale: Energy is sector leader (+27.8% YTD, +25.5% 6M) with Brent at $83; despite today's XLE -3.8% pullback, momentum remains strong and oil supports inflation hedge.

adjustments.action: Modestly increase AI/semi cap on tech momentum
adjustments.rationale: IT is 52W leader (+46.8%) and QQQ +2.6% today; positive yield curve and falling VIX support growth/duration assets, but PCE divergence caps conviction.

adjustments.action: Maintain gold allocation despite 18% drawdown from ATH
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4363 is 18% off ATH — the drawdown itself is not a buy signal given no crisis regime, but PCE/CPI divergence and geopolitical uncertainty warrant holding current allocation as insurance.

adjustments.action: Reduce healthcare cap given weak momentum
adjustments.rationale: Healthcare flagged as struggling sector by RADAR with negative daily performance; no defensive need in current non-recessionary environment.

adjustments.action: Maintain short-to-intermediate duration, slight TIPS tilt given PCE divergence
adjustments.rationale: PCE at 3.77% vs CPI 2.4% signals sticky services inflation; TIPS protect against upside inflation surprise while 5Y breakevens at 2.39% make TIPS fairly priced.

adjustments.action: Avoid high yield given tight spreads offering poor risk/reward
adjustments.rationale: HY spreads at 278bps are historically tight — asymmetric downside risk if growth slows; carry doesn't compensate for tail risk in Stable profile.
Financials +1.39% today and materials +1.98% show value rotation; dividend stocks provide income buffer if growth decelerates with PCE staying elevated.

adjustments.action: Maintain modest tactical cash given VIX 22 and mixed signals
adjustments.rationale: VIX at 22 (above calm but falling) with no clear regime = keep dry powder for potential dip-buying; T-bills at ~4% yield make cash productive, not punitive.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Neutral regime with PCE/CPI divergence favors short-to-intermediate duration with TIPS tilt; IG credit acceptable but HY offers poor risk/reward at 278bps spreads.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX 22 falling + mixed macro signals = modest cash as optionality; T-bills yielding ~4% make this productive positioning, not drag — ready to deploy on clarity or correction.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Falling VIX and risk-on equity momentum support crypto exposure in Agressif; capped below max given PCE uncertainty and neutral regime lacking full conviction.

warnings: PCE at 3.77% vs CPI 2.4% divergence is unusual — if PCE proves leading, Fed may turn hawkish despite market pricing no moves, creating a rates shock risk
HY spreads at 278bps are near cycle tights — any growth scare could trigger rapid widening (300-400bps) with outsized losses given low starting spread
Gold 18% off ATH could signal either healthy correction or early risk-off rotation — monitor if gold rebounds while equities stay flat (divergence = warning)
XLE -3.8% today despite Brent at $83 may signal sector rotation out of energy or demand concern — watch for follow-through
S&P at 7541 with strong daily moves (+1.8%) in a VIX 22 environment suggests positioning-driven rally — vulnerable to reversal on negative catalyst
EUR/USD at 1.162 with DTWEXBGS at 120 = dollar neutral-to-weak; further dollar weakness would boost EM and commodities but hurt US-centric portfolios

market_interpretation.geopolitical_risk: Moderate — Brent at $83 (down from 5d avg $89) suggests no acute energy shock, but gold at $4363 and VIX 22 indicate markets pricing some tail risk from unresolved geopolitical tensions.
